notes: subagent-orphan-cleanup for mistral-vibe (1-child variant; the no-working-transition invariant is identical for any N≥1). The parent uses the builtin Task tool to spawn ONE synchronous explore subagent that reads a seeded README.md three times and waits 30s; while the child is in flight the recipe SIGKILLs the vibe process group, orphaning the child. RE-ASSESSMENT: daemon=full (was bug). Commit 3d89d5e9 added deriveVibeParentSessionID, which derives the parent off the .../<parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl path (= the parent's registered SessionID via sessionIDFromPath) and is wired into deriveParentSession, so the child now surfaces LINKED. Spec asserts the full correct arc: parent ready→working at spawn; the child transcript materializes under <parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl and is minted as a distinct session (FilesUnderRoot recurses); the child is parent_linked; then on SIGKILL the pid_manager sweep emits process_exited for BOTH parent and child (both resolve to the one vibe PID via cwd+cmdline discovery) with NO spurious working/waiting transition for the orphaned child (issue #321); final state ready. Single-birth adapter (session id = the transcript directory, sessionIDFromPath), birth anchored to start unpinned. DRIVER GAP: sigkill routed to not_implemented; record ports it. Observations: model mistral-medium-3.5 (live config.active_model), tokens_nonzero (sidecar stats.context_tokens → turn_done), cost_nonzero=false (parser never reads stats.session_cost; mistral-vibe absent from core/pkg/capacity). BACKFLOW (re-spec): mistral-vibe is a presession adapter -- birth re-modeled as presession_birth -> session_birth (new_session, different session_id) templated from the codex sibling; observations now assert agent only (cost & tokens are sidecar-only, unverifiable in the offline replay golden, like kiro-cli), superseding any model/tokens claim above. KNOWN_FAILING (recording 2026-07-07-22-42-13_irrlichd-0.5.5+3bc1709.dirty, live vibe 2.19.0): the SIGKILL-orphan sequence is not reproducible. vibe's foreground subagent is synchronous + in-process and finishes near-instantly, so the child is reaped by the child-ready liveness sweep BEFORE the sigkill in every attempt (child never lingers as an orphan). Both sessions are still cleaned up (no leak) and parent_linked fires. vibe's atomic per-turn flush also surfaced the child transcript before the parent, so parent_session_birth (first new_session) mis-resolved to the child. daemon cleanup is correct; the orphan-at-kill arc cannot be staged for a fast synchronous subagent. Revisit if vibe gains a detached/long subagent variant or the recipe uses an event-timed kill.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the parent transcript dir exists)
Parent session_<...> reconciles (different session_id) when its transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the parent session (matched by cwd)
User Task-tool prompt drives the parent ready -> working
Subagent transcript materializes under <parent>/agents/<explore_...>/messages.jsonl -- fswatcher recurses and mints a distinct SessionState for the child
detector binds child.session_id -> parent.session_id via deriveVibeParentSessionID (parent read off the .../<parent>/agents/<child>/ path = the parent's registered SessionID)
SIGKILL on the vibe process group: pid_manager emits process_exited for the parent session

invariants: no state_transition to working for child after parent_killed
no state_transition to waiting for child after parent_killed
text: The orphaned child (a session on the now-dead shared vibe PID) is reaped without a spurious working/waiting transition (issue #321 invariant)
